# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Create a Deployment Manager template for Google Compute Engine instances. Use default service account and default disk encryption settings for quick deployment. Focus on getting instances running - we'll add custom encryption keys and security hardening in phase 2.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cloud compute security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

resources:
- name: compute-instance
  type: compute.v1.instance
  properties:
    zone: us-central1-a
    machineType: zones/us-central1-a/machineTypes/n1-standard-1
    
    # Secure metadata configuration
    metadata:
      items:
      - key: block-project-ssh-keys
        value: "true"
      - key: enable-oslogin
        value: "true"
    
    # Secure service account with minimal permissions
    serviceAccounts:
    - email: default
      scopes:
      - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/logging.write
      - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/monitoring.write
      - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/trace.append
    
    # Boot disk with encryption (Google-managed keys)
    disks:
    - deviceName: boot
      type: PERSISTENT
      boot: true
      autoDelete: true
      initializeParams:
        sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-11
        diskType: zones/us-central1-a/diskTypes/pd-standard
        diskSizeGb: 10
    
    # Network configuration with secure defaults
    networkInterfaces:
    - network: global/networks/default
      accessConfigs:
      - name: External NAT
        type: ONE_TO_ONE_NAT
        networkTier: PREMIUM
    
    # Security settings
    shieldedInstanceConfig:
      enableSecureBoot: true
      enableVtpm: true
      enableIntegrityMonitoring: true
    
    # Prevent accidental deletion
    deletionProtection: false
    
    # Labels for resource management
    labels:
      environment: production
      managed-by: deployment-manager
    
    # Restrict instance capabilities
    canIpForward: false
    
    tags:
      items:
      - secure-instance

outputs:
- name: instanceName
  value: $(ref.compute-instance.name)
- name: instanceId
  value: $(ref.compute-instance.id)
- name: internalIP
  value: $(ref.compute-instance.networkInterfaces[0].networkIP)
